routes.title: Join a session
routes.summary: Use the sessions hub if you want a practical, current route into Saturday CV support or youth club without sending a long message first.
routes.nextStep: Browse the sessions hub, check the current offer, and send a short enquiry if you want help choosing the right session.
routes.trustNote: Participants, parents, and referrers should be able to see timings, first-visit reassurance, and safeguarding context before contacting the team.
supportPoints: Current Saturday sessions live on the Sessions hub
First-time reassurance stays visible before someone attends
Contact remains available if you want to ask first

routes.title: Volunteer with us
routes.summary: Start here if you want role pathways, checks, and next-step expectations explained before you commit your time.
routes.nextStep: Use the volunteer route to understand the pathway, then send a short enquiry if the role feels like a fit.
routes.trustNote: Volunteering should feel fair, supported, and clear about safeguarding expectations rather than improvised.
supportPoints: Role pathways should be visible before any application flow
Safeguarding and checks stay visible early
A short enquiry should come before a long commitment

routes.title: Partner with us
routes.summary: Use this pathway for referrals, sponsorship, venue support, collaboration, or local partnership conversations that need a clearer public starting point.
routes.nextStep: Use the partner route to understand who it is for, what collaboration could look like, and how to send a short enquiry that can be routed properly.
routes.trustNote: Schools, funders, local authorities, and community organisations should be able to see a credible partnership invitation before they are asked to send a message.
supportPoints: Referrals, sponsorship, and collaboration all start here
A formal proposal is not required for first contact
The next step stays proportionate and enquiry-led

routes.title: Refer someone
routes.summary: Parents, carers, schools, and community referrers can start with a short explanation of who needs help and what kind of support might fit.
routes.nextStep: Use the contact route, choose a referral-focused reason, and tell us whether you are asking on someone else's behalf.
routes.trustNote: Referrers need a route that explains safety, fit, and next steps without forcing them through volunteer-first language.
supportPoints: You do not need to know the exact programme before asking
Parents, carers, schools, and partners can all start here
Safeguarding guidance stays visible if the question is about safety

routes.title: Support in another practical way
routes.summary: If you can offer space, introductions, practical help, or local support that does not fit a named role yet, start here.
routes.nextStep: Use a live public opportunity when one is published, or send a short message describing the support you have in mind.
routes.trustNote: The hub should stay open to practical help without turning every supporter into a fundraiser or sending them through the wrong route.
supportPoints: Current public opportunities should surface when they are live
Contact remains the fallback if your offer does not fit a named path
